keyMyths.title: Prince Siddhartha's Renunciation
keyMyths.description: Prince Siddhartha, sheltered from suffering, encounters old age, sickness, and death, prompting him to abandon his royal life and seek enlightenment. This pivotal moment marks the beginning of his journey towards becoming the Buddha.

keyMyths.title: The Great Departure
keyMyths.description: Siddhartha secretly leaves his palace at night, severing his ties to his family and kingdom, symbolizing his commitment to a life of asceticism and spiritual pursuit. He cuts off his hair and exchanges his royal robes for simple garments.

keyMyths.title: Mara's Temptation
keyMyths.description: As Siddhartha meditates under the Bodhi tree, Mara, the demon of illusion and death, attacks him with armies of monsters and seductive visions to prevent him from attaining enlightenment. Siddhartha resists all temptations, solidifying his resolve.

keyMyths.title: The First Sermon
keyMyths.description: After attaining enlightenment, the Buddha delivers his first sermon at Sarnath, setting in motion the Wheel of Dharma and teaching the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path. This marks the beginning of his public ministry.

keyMyths.title: Parinirvana
keyMyths.description: At the age of 80, the Buddha enters Parinirvana (final nirvana) in Kushinagar, peacefully passing away while surrounded by his disciples. His final teachings emphasize impermanence and the importance of self-reliance.

achievements: Attaining enlightenment under the Bodhi tree
Delivering the first sermon at Sarnath
Spreading the Dharma (Buddhist teachings) throughout India
Formulating the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path
Demonstrating compassion and wisdom in all his actions

sources.name: Dhammapada

sources.name: Tripitaka (Pali Canon)
sources.description: The standard collection of scriptures in the Theravada Buddhist tradition, comprising the Vinaya Pitaka, Sutta Pitaka, and Abhidhamma Pitaka.
